# The amyloid response across tau backgrounds, method by method {#sec-modality}

```{r}
#| label: modality-setup
#| include: false
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(ggplot2)
  library(patchwork)
})
options(warn = 2)                                    # promote any chunk warning to a render error (gate-critical)
targets::tar_source()                                # constants, plot theme + modality figure helpers
targets::tar_load(modality_scatter_figures)          # compact logFC pairs + functional group scores
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
theme_set(theme_tau())

msf <- modality_scatter_figures
```

## Per-method amyloid effect: tau-KO versus mutant-tau background

```{r}
#| label: fig-modality-amyloid-effect
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 10.4
#| fig-cap: "Per-method amyloid (NLGF) log2 fold change on the tau-KO background (NLGF_MAPTKI vs MAPTKI, y) against the mutant-tau background (NLGF_P301S vs P301S, x). The dashed line is y=x, the null of a tau-independent amyloid response; the tau-by-amyloid interaction contrast is x minus y, so distance from the diagonal ranks its magnitude. Phosphoproteomics points are parent-protein means of finite phosphosite logFC pairs. Labels mark empirical off-diagonal outliers using one standardized cutoff: unique display labels whose |x-y| exceeds the pooled 99.8th percentile of |x-y| across all four methods. The solid line is the OLS trend."
#| fig-alt: "Four scatter plots, one per method, each comparing the amyloid log fold change between the two tau backgrounds against a dashed identity line, with an OLS trend and empirical off-diagonal outliers labelled; phosphoproteomics is collapsed to parent-protein means."
panels <- lapply(msf$order, function(m) {
  modality_interaction_scatter(msf$panels[[m]]$data, title = msf$panels[[m]]$title)
})
wrap_plots(panels, ncol = 2)
```

## Off-diagonal functional categories

```{r}
#| label: fig-modality-functional-scores
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 8.2
#| fig-cap: "Standardized off-diagonal genes and proteins from Figure 6 assigned to one primary functional category per method. Broad role categories are assembled from mouse GO-BP annotations; threshold-passing items without a role-set hit are retained in explicit fallback categories rather than dropped. Phosphoproteomics categories use the same parent-protein mean points displayed in Figure 6. Each segment connects the aggregate amyloid log2 fold change in NLGF_MAPTKI to the aggregate amyloid log2 fold change in NLGF_P301S; segment colour is P301S minus MAPTKI, and point size is the number of scored genes or proteins in the category."
#| fig-alt: "Faceted dumbbell plots show modality-specific functional categories for standardized off-diagonal genes or proteins, with paired points for aggregate NLGF_MAPTKI and NLGF_P301S amyloid responses connected by a segment coloured by the P301S minus MAPTKI score."
functional_group_score_plot(msf$groups$summary, title = "Functional categories for standardized off-diagonal genes and proteins")
```
